response.justification: The candidate is a seasoned polyglot engineer with genuine developer-tooling credentials — he worked on the LabWindows/CVI IDE at National Instruments and has authored test runners, assertion libraries, and web frameworks — but his career centers on TypeScript/Node.js, DLang, and C/C++ rather than the JVM. The role's core requirements of deep Java and Kotlin expertise and solid Spring ecosystem experience are essentially absent: Java appears only as a secondary skill in past roles, and Kotlin, Spring, Hibernate, and application servers are not mentioned at all. Strong craftsmanship alone doesn't offset the missing domain stack for a JVM-tooling position.
strengths: Direct IDE development experience (NI LabWindows/CVI) and evident passion for language tooling
Long track record building complex systems, including high-throughput low-latency services
Strong software craftsmanship practices (TDD, CI/CD, open-source tooling authorship)
concerns: No Kotlin experience and only peripheral Java exposure
No Spring, Hibernate, or enterprise JVM framework background
Recent work is TypeScript/Node product engineering, far from JVM internals
